component.name: SONE
component.summary: Native Linux client for TIDAL — lossless and bit-perfect audio streaming

description.p: Stream your TIDAL library in lossless FLAC and Hi-Res quality up to 24-bit/192kHz — with the option to bypass your system mixer entirely for bit-perfect output.

A familiar, modern interface inspired by the streaming apps you already use. Exclusive ALSA mode bypasses PulseAudio and PipeWire entirely, sending the unaltered signal directly to your DAC for bit-perfect playback. Or use normal mode for casual listening through your system's default output.

ul.li: Lossless FLAC and Hi-Res streaming up to 24-bit/192kHz
Exclusive ALSA output for bit-perfect streaming to USB DACs
Volume normalization (ReplayGain) with automatic album/track switching
Scrobble to Last.fm, Libre.fm, and ListenBrainz with ISRC and MusicBrainz metadata
Synced lyrics display
Miniplayer — compact floating window with playback controls and resizable-adaptive layout
Full-screen player with album art, lyrics, and auto-hiding controls
Discord Rich Presence — show what you're listening to
Share and open tidal:// deep links for tracks, albums, playlists, artists, and mixes
Custom themes with full color picker
Queue persistence across restarts
MPRIS media controls and system tray
Proxy support — route traffic through HTTP, HTTPS, or SOCKS5 proxies

description.p: Unofficial client — requires an active TIDAL subscription. SONE is a streaming client only and does not support offline downloads.

screenshot.caption: Now playing drawer with play queue
Full-screen maximized player with auto-hiding controls and karaoke-style lyrics
Home page with TIDAL streaming library
Custom theme with full color customization

ul.li: Compact floating miniplayer with album art, playback controls, and vibrant color background
Responsive layout that adapts as you resize — drag and resize freely
Show what you're listening to on Discord — album art, track info, and a direct TIDAL link
Organize playlists into folders — create, rename, move, and delete
Edit playlist name, description, and toggle public/private visibility
Discover new music with recommended tracks at the bottom of playlists
"Playing from" label in the player bar shows your current listening source
Open tidal:// links directly in SONE with deep link support
Share tracks from the context menu
Bit-depth promotion in bit-perfect mode — lossless streams play at your DAC's native depth
Ctrl+E and Ctrl+B shortcuts to toggle exclusive and bit-perfect mode
MPRIS shuffle, repeat, and absolute seek support
Settings moved to a dedicated modal
Full light theme support — every page, component, menu, and overlay adapts to your chosen theme
Frosted glass effect for the maximized player in light mode
Wider page container and responsive grid that adapts from 1 to 6 columns
Adaptive and zoom-aware card scroll sections with scroll-snap and container queries
Replaced libappindicator tray with ksni for broader desktop compatibility
Single-instance support — launching SONE again focuses the existing window
Sort your favorites (albums, artists, mixes) by date added, name, or artist
Optimistic sidebar updates — album favorites, artist follows, and mix favorites reflect instantly
Track context menu on the player bar
Display all track artists and version suffixes across the entire UI
Consolidated mix and track radio into a single page
Smoother scrolling with frame-accurate position interpolation
Fixed context menu positioning across the app at non-default zoom levels
Fixed Wayland window controls becoming unresponsive after restoring from tray
Full-screen maximized player with album art, lyrics option, blurred background, and auto-hiding controls
Karaoke-style synced lyrics in a two-column layout with responsive font sizing
Instant track transitions with progressive album art loading
Smoother interactions — GPU compositing and optimized state management eliminate lag in the maximized view
HTTP, HTTPS, and SOCKS5 proxy support with a new settings UI — all traffic routes through your configured proxy
Exclusive mode now probes your DAC's supported formats and sample rates, avoiding mismatches and falling back gracefully
Toast notifications when exclusive mode resamples audio or encounters unsupported sample rates
Playback stops cleanly on network errors instead of draining the queue, with user-friendly error toasts
ALSA writer respawns on device change — hot-swapping DACs no longer requires a restart
Tray icon and global shortcuts no longer prevent startup if unsupported
Scrobble your listening history to Last.fm, Libre.fm, and ListenBrainz — complete with ISRC and MusicBrainz metadata
Toggle window decorations to switch between native and custom title bar
Repeat and shuffle now work correctly — wrap-around, repeat-one, and shuffle-aware playback all fixed
Fixed rapid track skipping causing the UI to freeze
User menu reorganized into clean, grouped sections
